meaning: n.(扈晉Ο)霓ｯ譛ｨ譚ｿ,霓ｯ譛ｨ蜈ｬ蜻頑攸
examples: That's why the internet is so much more fun than reading the corkboard at your laundromat.
There is a certain relationship between thermal conductivity and density of corkboard.
Not only does the corkboard provide you with some history, it's also a window into the future.
He caught sight of a large corkboard tacked over with hand-printed messages, a community bulletin board.
There is a picture of it on the corkboard in his room, the fish in the net, the lure bristling in its crude mouth.
